Her teaching style can be controversial- if you ask for her help and she doesn't know it, you are on your own. Turn your assignments in! period. She is hypocritically strict, she can be late in grading our hw, but will not accept late assignments, EVEN if it was 1 minute late...
A personable, and helpful teacher.  Will help if you ask.  Tries to connect material to actual, real life application.  Whichever school has her as a professor will be lucky, students will be lucky.

Would I take it again? Heck no. Unnecessary workload, don't even know why my major (HAPP) need me to take this class. Busy work TEXTBOOK IS YOUR FRIEND in this class, honestly. Follow the book and you'll be fine. The prof can be a pain when it comes late assignments (hypocritical). She can be 2weeks behind in grading, but won't accept late hw...
No Test or Exam Just weekly projects inform of homework easy though just follow the textbook steps. Shes really good and fair with gradding. Talk to her if you have any problem she will help

analysis.name: Jay Unick
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: J is hilarious! He curses a lot and makes research kinda fun. He is aware of how boring it can be but attempts to make a fun activity.  Grades based on a midterm, a final, and a research paper that you write in sections starting the 3rd wk of class. He is very approachable. Exams are a lil tough but you can def get A if u pay attn and go to class

Very intelligent and knowledgeable about the subject.  Teaches all chapters thoroughly via powerpoints and is very clear about what he will test on.  One paper that is done in four sections, fairly easy grader.  Would recommend this professor.
Intelligent and organized presentation of material. On top that, great sense of humor. He also gives interesting examples to hammer home concepts.  He's basically the man.

Had low expectations coming out of HB1 in terms of staying interested and alert but Jay made it really great.  Very smart and had a lot of knowledge to share and did so in an interesting, informative way.  Included videos which were illustrative.  Love love love his self-depricating sense of humor, its funny w/o being pathetic.  Great Prof recommen
Great teacher!  loved his class despite low interest in topic. already signed up with him next semester.

analysis.name: Yolanda Valencia
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I can tell the professor is a kind, compassionate person and is passionate about her lectures. She encourages critical thinking and ties what we're learning to real world events. Minimal work and clear instructions on assignments and projects.
She must have improved since these ratings because this class may have been the easiest class I have taken. A LOT of homework, but I would suggest you use "outside" help and you will be fine.

BEWARE IF YOU HAVE ACCOMMODATIONS. I wish I never enrolled in this class. She ignored both of my accommodation letters from the school and fought with me when I would try to use them. She would also make mean and backhanded comments about how I needed accommodations, not only to me via email, but to the whole class every time I had an issue.

The course is easy but arduous. There is a lot of busy work. She asks that you watch documentaries and read the textbook and articles. The class isn't hard, but Prof Valencia makes it hard and hard to attend. Lectures are boring and confusing; there is no clarity on what we need to take notes on and what we don't.

Middle of the semester, she randomly implemented a participation grade that wasn't in the syllabus previously. Not sure if she's allowed to do that. The assignments and exams are easy, but they're kinda common sense if you've ever taken human geo classes in HS, so people didnt rly go to her lectures

analysis.name: Ramana Vinjamuri
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: ONE OF THE BEST PROFESSORS OF ALL TIME. ONLY TAKE 411 WITH THIS MAN. IF YOU HAVE TO DELAY IT A SEMESTER, THEN DO IT. IF YOU COME TO CLASS AND DO THE WORK YOU WILL GET AN A. best professor of all time. also he is super passionate abt his work.

He is the most likeable professor I've had at UMBC so far. He's extremely lenient but at the same time he wants each specific student to be retaining the content being taught in class. He is very knowledgeable about the field and has good experience with his own labs. If CS 411 with him is available, he is the best option.

Best class I have ever taken. All I had to do was to show up to class and follow the lectures. The HW are really easy if you follow the lectures and even if you don't you can just look at the slides. The midterm and the final were both take home and we had 2 weeks to do it.
I can count with one hand all the good comp sci professors at umbc, and he's one of them!!!

One of the best teachers I've had at umbc. He cares about student learning and tries to get everyone to participate. Slides are informative and exams where entirely online and open book. Great class, great professor 10/10 would take again.

analysis.name: Vickie Williams
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 40 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: truly the most caring prof. i accidentally missed a 100 point assignment, making my final grade a B, but she let me turn in the assignment a month after the semester ended, and changed my final grade to an A. because you have to take prac 096 with this course, she doesnt give too many assignments, with the most difficult being a presentation.

Dr. Williams is a very sweet lady. She tries to make the course as easy and clear as possible and is one of the fairest and most lenient graders out there. My only two gripes is that EDUC 311 is basically a rehash of PSYC 100, and the weekly online modules are frustrating: though easy, take hours to do (with essays!) but only worth 35 points each.

attendance is graded but classes are hybrid. we used class time to learn about education in baltimore & work on group projects. class always finished early & i usually used the time as a free period. she's a rlly easy grader, super easy to get an A.

Dr. Williams, is an outstanding professor. She wants 's you to succeed in her classes. She does grade work late sometimes, and is very accommodating towards the students who missed the previous lectures! The course load can be very light depending on how you manage your time. Quizzes aren't complicated as long as you do the readings before class.
She is so amazing and was really understanding I am definitely going to sign up for one of her other classes in the future.

analysis.name: Caitlin Varisco
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: She made students purchase a $75-$110 textboook and assigned only like 10 pages through the ENTIRE semester. Although it was first time teaching nutrition, I feel like I was forced to memorize her slides instead of actually learning. Quizzes and exams were often pulled straight from her slides where she did not explain it in her lecture vids.

Varisco relied on dense slides-reading directly from them with little explanation&made class feel overwhelming, w/ quiz/exam based on irrelevant info. Nutrition should be engaging,but wasn't.The course felt unproductive, with memorization over understanding.The groupproject was also weird-analyzing students' eating habits.

Very saddening! Often fails to grade assignments on time. Some of the assignments were ungraded well after the semester ended. Barely replies to the emails, even though the course is completely online. The course is poorly designed. She blamed the wrong grade being displayed throughout the semester due to a Blackboard error, and did not fix it.

This is my first time taking this professor and had a great experience. The class is a 400 level hybrid 6 week course on antibiotics. Despite the quick session the class was extremely easy. Prof. Varisco is an easy grader as long as you follow the syllabus. She does give extra credit opportunities and makeup assignments with proper documentation.

BIOL 101 not 233. Not sure what the other reviews are getting at—Professor Varisco is one of the best professors I've had at UMBC. She's incredibly kind, supportive, and genuinely cares about her students' success. She provides lots of extra credit opportunities and she's always willing to meet outside of class if you're struggling.

analysis.name: Kathy Vu
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Great professor who is easy to connect with, outlines clear expectations and is willing to help if you're confused. Although research methods isn't my favorite psych course, she made it enjoyable and easy to get a good grade in her class. Tests are open book (easy to pass if you study) and she encouraged groups to work together on assignments.

Everything is online, including the exams, which are on a lockdown browser, no camera though. Very nice, responds to emails fast, posts lectures on Blackboard, and honeslty no complaints about her teaching style.

Her teaching style is 5/10. As such, lectures are hard to get through but luckily not mandatory.She is very considerate and gives lot of opportunities to get a good grade. The main downside is that she use Respondus lockdown for exams (no audio/visual tho). The exam 1 can be googled but not 2 and 3 and is opposite from what's in the text.Good class

analysis.name: Christelle Viauroux
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 60 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Very. Very. Very. Very. Very. Very. Very. Very. BAD. Im taking the time out of my day to do this. Just dont take this class. If you need it and shes the only professor, take it the next semester. Its not worth it. Just dont take it with her. Im so serious.. DONT. Youre probably still thinking it must not be that bad. But im here to tell you, it is.

This is supposed to be a 101 course. It is surely not taught like so. Extremely fast pace, difficult tests and quizzes. Learned more in a 1 hour review session with the TA than I did the past 2 months with the professor. The best part is, I took this class as GEP credit and I wish I had taken something else. This class is beyond stressful.

If you are planning on taking econ as a gen ed or even as part of your major DO NOT TAKE THIS PROFESSOR. She does not know how to teach, never holds review sessions, does not provide accurate information about what will be on exams. Don't even try it drop the class or find another professor. NO CAP!!!!

In class assignments are a lot and she only grades them out of 1 point. She expects for you to finish a 5 page assignment before the end of class and at best she will give you maybe 10-15 minutes to finish it before the end of class. Homework assignments are very frustrating and it's hard to follow along with her in class.

The worst teacher that I have ever had in my 3 years of being in college. Take a 100 level course and treats it as a 400 level course. Tests are hard, she's very unorganized and frankly I don't think she really knows what she is talking about when she is teaching. Worst teacher at UMBC. Avoid at all costs.

analysis.name: Virginia  Estabrook
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Estabrook is a great professor, and this is a pretty easy class. Attendance is mandatory and graded. You don't really have to pay attention or take notes to be successful. There's a homework assignment every week; they're easy, but need to be handwritten/printed out. Everything on the exams is literally homework questions verbatim.

Estabrook is a good and passionate professor, if you like the subject, she is good.

If taking for Elective credits, it has a fairly large amount of work for a 100 level, and has mandatory attendance, still not a bad option.

She's very eager to teach students, but also to make sure that they are learning and understand the material. Her lectures were hilarious—they had me chuckling a lot, even if the rest of the class was silent. Her syllabus scared me a little when I read it before the first class, but she's very nice and cares a lot about her work and her students.
Lectures were so boring but work was manageable as long as you do the 4 weekly readings that are 60 pages long. Allows index cards for 2 in-person exams. Does not enter grades until after finals.

Professor Estabrook is friendly and upbeat. Her lectures are easy to understand and her slides are very helpful. Her study guides are fantastic for preparing students for the three exams. Sometimes, her classes run a few minutes past the end time. The weekly homework assignments are easy but quite tedious as she has students handwrite them.

analysis.name: Lisa Vetter
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 36 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: One of the worst professors I have ever had, including high school. Her ideas are incredibly inflexible and she refuses to engage in any debate. While in-class discussion is encouraged, it rarely stays on topic. If a topic is, eventually, broached, she often takes over and dominates the conversation. She has no respect for her students.

I enjoyed Prof. Vetter's class, her grading scale makes it difficult to get an A, but not impossible. Exam average must remain a 90% or above, which isn't an easy task. Other than that, the lectures and reading are interesting if you are someone who is interested in political science. If you arent passionate about the subject, might not be for you.

Very confusing grading system that makes little to no sense. She is not very tech savvy, which leads to posting assignments late, sometimes the day they are due, and does not announce when something is posted. Very unapproachable outside of class, and will reply to emails very passive-aggressively, and offers little to no help.

Dr. Vetter uses a team-based learning approach, which is the only thing that saved me from a C.  If you have a good group, you'll do fine.  If you don't...good luck.  Lectures after the quizzes are boring.  Very limited interaction with class and is not flexible at all w/ syllabus.  She's not "avoid at all costs", but not highly recommended either.

Likely the worst professor I have ever had. Our only contact with Professor Vetter was pre-recorded lecture videos from a previous semester and half hour weekly meetings to which she was often late or canceled all together. I have no idea what she did outside of class but her time certainly wasn't spent putting an ounce of effort into her class.
